input.title: Ipamorelin stimulates growth hormone release in healthy adults: a pilot dose-escalation study
input.abstract: Ten healthy male volunteers (aged 22–35) received single subcutaneous injections of ipamorelin at 1, 10, 30, and 100 µg/kg in a four-period crossover design with one-week washout between periods. Peak serum GH (Cmax) increased dose-dependently: 2.1, 8.4, 18.7, and 24.3 ng/mL at the four doses respectively. IGF-1 was not significantly elevated at any single dose. No effects on cortisol, ACTH, or prolactin were observed. All doses were well tolerated; mild transient headache was reported by 3 participants at the highest dose. This pilot study supports the GH-selective profile of ipamorelin and informs dosing for future longer-duration studies.
